titleStmt.title: 193. Memorandum From the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) to Secretary of State ) to Secretary of State Vance

div.head: 193. Memorandum From the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) to Secretary of State Vance Source: Carter Library, National Security Affairs, Brzezinski Material; Country File, Box 88, Zimbabwe (Rhodesia): 1–4/78. Secret; Sensitive.
div.p: As a result of our conversation with the President today, the following decisions on Rhodesia were taken:According to the President’s Daily Diary, Carter met with Mondale, Vance, Young, Brzezinski, Aaron, and Jordan from 2 to 3 p.m in the Oval Office. (Carter Library, Presidential Materials)

1. The United States should seek the convening of a conference of the nationalists, the front-line countries, and—if possible—Smith to try to bring agreement among them on the basis of the Anglo-American plan, incorporating the positive features of the Salisbury settlement.See Document 192.
2. We will not support the Salisbury settlements nor condemn them. We will remain committed to the Anglo-American plan, and seek to bring the Salisbury settlement into conformity with it.

3. We will not support a veto by the British of the Nigerian Resolution.Reference is presumably to UNSC Resolution 423 (1978), adopted March 14, by a vote of 10 to 0 with 5 abstentions (including the United Kingdom, the United States, France, Germany, and Canada). (Yearbook of the United Nations, 1978, p. 227) We will abstain with an explanation of our vote. However, we should also work to develop a resolution which all can support.
4. We should go forward with planning for a May visit from President Kaunda of Zambia.
The Department of State should prepare a country paper on Zambia, outlining its economic and military needs and the extent to which U.S. assistance might be made available.
